preamble: The shift from the voiced labiodental fricative [v] to the glottal stop [ʔ] is a form of debuccalization and final lenition, primarily observed in word-final positions or as a result of complete oral constriction loss in specific language families.

examples.note: Regular shift where word-final bilabial or labiodental sounds debuccalize to a glottal stop.
Occasional glottal reinforcement or replacement of final fricatives in rapid speech or specific regional varieties.

sources: Phonological research on Mayan consonant shifts.
